title: fix _bd_dolt_push_smoke_test to use the real bd dolt push contract
description: GREEN for tmpl-8z1 / scenario 62eb2a8b9b617f4b. Unblocks from RED (tmpl-8z1.1). In cli.py configure the Dolt remote (bd dolt remote add) using the rendered remote URL, run bare 'bd dolt push' (or --remote <name>), check the real exit code, exit bootstrap non-zero with a diagnostic on real failure.

title: write the failing test asserting the REAL bd dolt push contract for the bootstrap smoke-test
description: RED for tmpl-8z1 / scenario 62eb2a8b9b617f4b. Rewrite the conftest step + bd-shim so they assert the real bd contract: bootstrap configures a Dolt remote via 'bd dolt remote add <name> <url>' then runs bare 'bd dolt push' (no positional URL), checks the REAL exit code, and the shim REJECTS the old ['bd','dolt','push',<url>] positional shape. Must fail against the current no-op impl.

title: bootstrap bd dolt push smoke-test (62eb2a8b9b617f4b) invokes a non-existent CLI shape and is a guaranteed no-op against real bd
description: Reviewer (lead-wnps) BLOCK. _bd_dolt_push_smoke_test in src/shop_templates/cli.py invokes ['bd','dolt','push',remote] passing the rendered sync.remote URL as a positional argv token. Real 'bd dolt push' takes NO positional (Usage: bd dolt push [flags]; only --remote <named-remote>, which takes a remote NAME not a URL). Verified empirically: 'bd dolt push <url>' silently ignores the positional, pushes to the DEFAULT remote, and when none is configured prints 'No remote is configured - skipping' and EXITS 0. So the smoke-test always reports success and would NEVER catch a misconfigured/unreachable remote - defeating scenario 62eb2a8b9b617f4b's stated purpose. Compounding: behavior A writes a 'sync.remote' YAML key that bd does not read (bd uses 'bd dolt remote add <name> <url>' / Remotes section, not a sync.remote config key); bd's prefix key is 'issue-prefix' (hyphen) not the written 'issue_prefix'. Tests pass only because the bd-shim accepts any argv and the Then-step asserts the wrong contract (expected_remote in argv). Fix: implementer must invoke the real CLI - configure the Dolt remote (bd dolt remote add) and run 'bd dolt push' (or 'bd dolt push --remote <name>'), and rewrite the step to assert the real contract. Every other 'bd dolt push' usage in this repo (AGENTS.md, .beads/README.md, canonical bc.md/bc-router) uses bare 'bd dolt push'.
close_reason: Reviewer (lead-wnps re-review) confirms the defect is resolved. _bd_dolt_push_smoke_test now (1) configures the Dolt remote via 'bd dolt remote add origin <url>', (2) runs bare 'bd dolt push' (no positional URL), (3) checks the REAL subprocess exit code, (4) returns rc so bootstrap exits non-zero with a stderr diagnostic on failure. Cross-checked against real bd 1.0.3 'bd dolt push --help' (no positional; --remote is a NAME) and 'bd dolt remote --help' (add <name> <url>). Tests now assert the real contract and reject the old 'bd dolt push <url>' shape (conftest shim exit 2 on positional + Then-step AssertionError); failure path drives a real non-zero via injection marker after a remote is configured. Full-suite failure-set diff vs base 46c33c6 is empty (22==22 identical). Sub-issues tmpl-8z1.1/.2 closed.

title: write failing test: bin/shop-shell guards the agent-vault CA (self-source + fail-loud)
description: RED for tmpl-dfn / scenario @2adc62a25c401e4b. Test asserts the bootstrap-poured bin/shop-shell body: (a) when AGENT_VAULT_CA_PEM empty/unset, self-sources CA via literal 'agent-vault ca fetch' OR reads host file 'agent-vault-ca.pem'; (b) passes CA into container via AGENT_VAULT_CA_PEM on the docker run; (c) if CA still unobtainable, exits non-zero with diagnostic and never reaches docker run. Additive to scenario 172 (5335c39eb06f7493) which must still hold.
